background.key_experience: Got fired from a retail job at 23 when facial recognition software flagged her as 'unproductive' during a 3-minute bathroom break that violated the algorithm's productivity metrics - now deeply distrusts workplace surveillance tech

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (anxious about social rejection)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new ideas)

cognition.core_value: Fairness in treatment - everyone should get the same chances she didn't get
cognition.bias: Bandwagon effect - immediately adopts whatever opinion is trending in her TikTok feed about tech companies, triggered when she sees multiple creators saying the same thing

current_state.recent_memory: Yesterday her delivery app added a new 'customer satisfaction prediction' feature that docked her pay because an algorithm predicted a customer might give less than 5 stars

online_persona.typical_behavior: Lurks for hours reading comments before posting, then parrots whatever anti-tech sentiment is currently viral, deletes posts if they get downvoted
online_persona.opinion_style: Uses personal anecdotes as universal truths ('This happened to me so it's happening to everyone'), heavily influenced by whichever influencer she watched most recently
hot_takes: All algorithms are designed to screw over workers
If it's on an app, it's probably spying on you
Tech CEOs should have to work delivery jobs for a month
